core_ideas: All solid objects deform elastically when force is applied to them, up to a point.
Different objects have a different elastic limit, which is the maximum amount of deformation they can withstand, beyond which they will deform permanently.
Different objects have a different breaking point, which is the maximum amount of deformation they can withstand, beyond which they will crack or split apart.
The type of material, the shape, and the thickness of an object all affect how much it deforms when a force is applied to it.

misconceptions.1-a: If it bends, it must be damaged (permanent). If it 'looks normal,' no deformation happened.
misconceptions.1-b: Same force always means the same deformation regardless of area.
misconceptions.2-a: Elastic limit is an amount of force (or a visible bend), not a material threshold.
misconceptions.3-a: The thicker the object is, the more challenging it is to be broken.
misconceptions.3-b: Breaking point is how much something bends.
misconceptions.4-a: If force applied is not enough, for some objects, they won't deform.

quiz_items.stem: A plastic spoon is bent. At first it springs back. If bent farther, it stays bent. If bent even farther, it snaps. Which statement best matches the terms?

options.A: The elastic limit is when it snaps.
options.B: The breaking point is when it first bends.
options.C: The breaking point is when it stays bent.
options.D: Elastic limit is where it won't spring back; breaking point is where it snaps.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?

Two rulers are the same length but made of different materials. You push down on the center of each ruler with the same force and then let go. Ruler A springs back. Ruler B stays slightly bent. Which conclusion is best?

options.A: Ruler B did not deform because the force was not strong enough.
options.B: The same force pushed Ruler B past its elastic limit, so it deformed permanently.
options.C: Elastic limit is the amount of force you used, so both rulers have the same elastic limit.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?
You push on a thick wooden table with your finger. The table does not appear to move or change shape. Which statement is scientifically accurate?

options.A: No deformation occurred because the table looks exactly the same as before.
options.B: The table deformed elastically, even if the change was too small for your eyes to see.
options.C: Deformation only begins once the force is strong enough to leave a permanent dent.
options.D: Solid objects like wood are too hard to deform unless they reach their breaking point.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?
Two beams are made of the same plastic. Beam X is twice as thick as Beam Y. If you apply the same amount of force to both, which will happen?

options.A: Both deform the same because the same force is applied.
options.B: Beam X deforms more because the same force is applied over a smaller contact area.
options.C: Beam Y deforms more because it is thinner.
options.D: Neither deforms unless the force is large enough to crack the plastic.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?
